NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely is Your tabernacle, O LORD of hosts!
NKJV.2: My soul longs, yes, even faints For the courts of the LORD; My heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.
NKJV.3: Even the sparrow has found a home, And the swallow a nest for herself, Where she may lay her young— Even Your altars, O LORD of hosts, My King and my God.
NKJV.4: Blessed are those who dwell in Your house; They will still be praising You. Selah
NKJV.5: Blessed is the man whose strength is in You, Whose heart is set on pilgrimage.
NKJV.6: As they pass through the Valley of Baca, They make it a spring; The rain also covers it with pools.
NKJV.7: They go from strength to strength; Each one appears before God in Zion.
NKJV.8: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; Give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
NKJV.9: O God, behold our shield, And look upon the face of Your anointed.
NKJV.10: For a day in Your courts is better than a thousand. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God Than dwell in the tents of wickedness.
NKJV.11: For the LORD God is a sun and shield; The LORD will give grace and glory; No good thing will He withhold From those who walk uprightly.
NKJV.12: O LORD of hosts, Blessed is the man who trusts in You!

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Gittith<S>1665</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> for the sons<S>1121</S> of Korah<S>7141</S>. How amiable<S>3039</S> are thy tabernacles<S>4908</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>! <sup>for the sons: or, of the sons</sup>

KJV.4: Blessed<S>835</S> are they that dwell<S>3427</S> in thy house<S>1004</S>: they will be still praising<S>1984</S> thee. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.5: Blessed<S>835</S> is the man<S>120</S> whose strength<S>5797</S> is in thee; in whose heart<S>3824</S> are the ways<S>4546</S> of them.
KJV.6: Who passing<S>5674</S> through the valley<S>6010</S> of Baca<S>1056</S> <S>1057</S> make<S>7896</S> it a well<S>4599</S>; the rain<S>4175</S> also filleth<S>5844</S> the pools<S>1293</S>. <sup>Baca: or, mulberry trees make him a well, etc</sup> <sup>filleth: Heb. covereth</sup>
KJV.7: They go<S>3212</S> from strength<S>2428</S> to strength<S>2428</S>, every one of them in Zion<S>6726</S> appeareth<S>7200</S> before God<S>430</S>. <sup>strength to: or, company to company</sup>
KJV.8: O LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, hear<S>8085</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>: give ear<S>238</S>, O God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.

KJV.10: For a day<S>3117</S> in thy courts<S>2691</S> is better<S>2896</S> than a thousand<S>505</S>. I had rather<S>977</S> be a doorkeeper<S>5605</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of my God<S>430</S>, than to dwell<S>1752</S> in the tents<S>168</S> of wickedness<S>7562</S>. <sup>I had: Heb. I would choose rather to sit at the threshold</sup>
KJV.11: For the LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> is a sun<S>8121</S> and shield<S>4043</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> will give<S>5414</S> grace<S>2580</S> and glory<S>3519</S>: no good<S>2896</S> thing will he withhold<S>4513</S> from them that walk<S>1980</S> uprightly<S>8549</S>.

ESV.1: How lovely is your dwelling place, O LORD of hosts!
ESV.2: My soul longs, yes, faints for the courts of the LORD; my heart and flesh sing for joy to the living God.
ESV.3: Even the sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, at your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God.
ESV.4: Blessed are those who dwell in your house, ever singing your praise! Selah
ESV.5: Blessed are those whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways to Zion.
ESV.6: As they go through the Valley of Baca they make it a place of springs; the early rain also covers it with pools.
ESV.7: They go from strength to strength; each one appears before God in Zion.
ESV.8: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
ESV.9: Behold our shield, O God; look on the face of your anointed!
ESV.10: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of wickedness.
ESV.11: For the LORD God is a sun and shield; the LORD bestows favor and honor. No good thing does he withhold from those who walk uprightly.
ESV.12: O LORD of hosts, blessed is the one who trusts in you!

NASB.1: For the choir director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely are Your dwelling places, O LORD of hosts!
NASB.2: My soul longed and even yearned for the courts of the LORD; My heart and my flesh sing for joy to the living God.
NASB.3: The bird also has found a house, And the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, Even Your altars, O LORD of hosts, My King and my God.
NASB.4: How blessed are those who dwell in Your house! They are ever praising You. Selah.
NASB.5: How blessed is the man whose strength is in You, In whose heart are the highways [to Zion]!
NASB.6: Passing through the valley of Baca they make it a spring; The early rain also covers it with blessings.
NASB.7: They go from strength to strength, [Every one of them] appears before God in Zion.
NASB.8: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; Give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah.
NASB.9: Behold our shield, O God, And look upon the face of Your anointed.
NASB.10: For a day in Your courts is better than a thousand [outside]. I would rather stand at the threshold of the house of my God Than dwell in the tents of wickedness.
NASB.11: For the LORD God is a sun and shield; The LORD gives grace and glory; No good thing does He withhold from those who walk uprightly.
NASB.12: O LORD of hosts, How blessed is the man who trusts in You!

NLT.1:
How lovely is your dwelling place,
O LORD of Heaven’s Armies.
NLT.2:
I long, yes, I faint with longing
to enter the courts of the LORD.
With my whole being, body and soul,
I will shout joyfully to the living God.
NLT.3:
Even the sparrow finds a home,
and the swallow builds her nest and raises her young
at a place near your altar,
O LORD of Heaven’s Armies, my King and my God!
NLT.4:
What joy for those who can live in your house,
always singing your praises. Interlude
NLT.5:
What joy for those whose strength comes from the LORD,
who have set their minds on a pilgrimage to Jerusalem.
NLT.6:
When they walk through the Valley of Weeping,
it will become a place of refreshing springs.
The autumn rains will clothe it with blessings.
NLT.7:
They will continue to grow stronger,
and each of them will appear before God in Jerusalem.
NLT.8:
O LORD God of Heaven’s Armies, hear my prayer.
Listen, O God of Jacob. Interlude
NLT.9:
O God, look with favor upon the king, our shield!
Show favor to the one you have anointed.
NLT.10:
A single day in your courts
is better than a thousand anywhere else!
I would rather be a gatekeeper in the house of my God
than live the good life in the homes of the wicked.
NLT.11:
For the LORD God is our sun and our shield.
He gives us grace and glory.
The LORD will withhold no good thing
from those who do what is right.
NLT.12:
O LORD of Heaven’s Armies,
what joy for those who trust in you.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. How lovely are your dwellings, Yahweh of Armies!
WEB.2: My soul longs, and even faints for the courts of Yahweh. My heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.
WEB.3: Yes, the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may have her young, near your altars, Yahweh of Armies, my King, and my God.
WEB.4: Blessed are those who dwell in your house. They are always praising you. Selah.
WEB.5: Blessed are those whose strength is in you; who have set their hearts on a pilgrimage.
WEB.6: Passing through the valley of Weeping, they make it a place of springs. Yes, the autumn rain covers it with blessings.
WEB.7: They go from strength to strength. Everyone of them appears before God in Zion.
WEB.8: Yahweh, God of Armies, hear my prayer. Listen, God of Jacob. Selah.
WEB.9: Behold, God our shield, look at the face of your anointed.
WEB.10: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.
WEB.11: For Yahweh God is a sun and a shield. Yahweh will give grace and glory. He withholds no good thing from those who walk blamelessly.
WEB.12: Yahweh of Armies, blessed is the man who trusts in you.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to a Philistine lute. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely are Your dwelling places,
O LORD of hosts!
AMP.2:
My soul (my life, my inner self) longs for and greatly desires the courts of the LORD;
My heart and my flesh sing for joy to the living God.
AMP.3:
The bird has found a house,
And the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young—
Even Your altars, O LORD of hosts,
My King and my God.
AMP.4:
Blessed and greatly favored are those who dwell in Your house and Your presence;
They will be singing Your praises all the day long. Selah.
AMP.5:
Blessed and greatly favored is the man whose strength is in You,
In whose heart are the highways to Zion.
AMP.6:
Passing through the Valley of Weeping ( Baca), they make it a place of springs;
The early rain also covers it with blessings.
AMP.7:
They go from strength to strength [increasing in victorious power];
Each of them appears before God in Zion.
AMP.8:
O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer;
Listen, O God of Jacob! Selah.
AMP.9:
See our shield, O God,
And look at the face of Your anointed [the king as Your representative].
AMP.10:
For a day in Your courts is better than a thousand [anywhere else];
I would rather stand [as a doorkeeper] at the threshold of the house of my God
Than to live [at ease] in the tents of wickedness.
AMP.11:
For the LORD God is a sun and shield;
The LORD bestows grace and favor and honor;
No good thing will He withhold from those who walk uprightly.
AMP.12:
O LORD of hosts,
How blessed and greatly favored is the man who trusts in You [believing in You, relying on You, and committing himself to You with confident hope and expectation].

MSG.1:
A Korah psalm
What a beautiful home, God of the Angel Armies!
I've always longed to live in a place like this,
MSG.2:
Always dreamed of a room in your house,
where I could sing for joy to God-alive!
MSG.3:
Birds find nooks and crannies in your house,
sparrows and swallows make nests there.
They lay their eggs and raise their young,
singing their songs in the place where we worship.
God of the Angel Armies! King! God!
MSG.4: How blessed they are to live and sing there!
MSG.5:
And how blessed all those in whom you live,
whose lives become roads you travel;
MSG.6:
They wind through lonesome valleys, come upon brooks,
discover cool springs and pools brimming with rain!
MSG.7:
God-traveled, these roads curve up the mountain, and
at the last turn—Zion! God in full view!
MSG.8:
God of the Angel Armies, listen:
O God of Jacob, open your ears—I'm praying!
MSG.9:
Look at our shields, glistening in the sun,
our faces, shining with your gracious anointing.
MSG.10:
One day spent in your house, this beautiful place of worship,
beats thousands spent on Greek island beaches.
I'd rather scrub floors in the house of my God
than be honored as a guest in the palace of sin.
MSG.11:
All sunshine and sovereign is God,
generous in gifts and glory.
He doesn't scrimp with his traveling companions.
MSG.12: It's smooth sailing all the way with God of the Angel Armies.

NIV.1:
Psalm 84
For the director of music. According to gittith. Of the Sons of Korah. A psalm.
How lovely is your dwelling place,
O Lord Almighty!
NIV.2:
My soul yearns, even faints,
for the courts of the Lord;
my heart and my flesh cry out
for the living God.
NIV.3:
Even the sparrow has found a home,
and the swallow a nest for herself,
where she may have her young—
a place near your altar,
O Lord Almighty, my King and my God.
NIV.4:
Blessed are those who dwell in your house;
they are ever praising you. Selah
NIV.5:
Blessed are those whose strength is in you,
who have set their hearts on pilgrimage.
NIV.6:
As they pass through the Valley of Baca,
they make it a place of springs;
the autumn rains also cover it with pools.
NIV.7:
They go from strength to strength,
till each appears before God in Zion.
NIV.8:
Hear my prayer, O Lord God Almighty;
listen to me, O God of Jacob. Selah
NIV.9:
Look upon our shield, O God;
look with favor on your anointed one.
NIV.10:
Better is one day in your courts
than a thousand elsewhere;
I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God
than dwell in the tents of the wicked.
NIV.11:
For the Lord God is a sun and shield;
the Lord bestows favor and honor;
no good thing does he withhold
from those whose walk is blameless.
NIV.12:
O Lord Almighty,
blessed is the man who trusts in you.

NRSVCE.1:
How lovely is your dwelling place,
O LORD of hosts!
NRSVCE.2:
My soul longs, indeed it faints
for the courts of the LORD;
my heart and my flesh sing for joy
to the living God.
NRSVCE.3:
Even the sparrow finds a home,
and the swallow a nest for herself,
where she may lay her young,
at your altars, O LORD of hosts,
my King and my God.
NRSVCE.4:
Happy are those who live in your house,
ever singing your praise. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
Happy are those whose strength is in you,
in whose heart are the highways to Zion.
NRSVCE.6:
As they go through the valley of Baca
they make it a place of springs;
the early rain also covers it with pools.
NRSVCE.7:
They go from strength to strength;
the God of gods will be seen in Zion.
NRSVCE.8:
O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer;
give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
NRSVCE.9:
Behold our shield, O God;
look on the face of your anointed.
NRSVCE.10:
For a day in your courts is better
than a thousand elsewhere.
I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God
than live in the tents of wickedness.
NRSVCE.11:
For the LORD God is a sun and shield;
he bestows favor and honor.
No good thing does the LORD withhold
from those who walk uprightly.
NRSVCE.12:
O LORD of hosts,
happy is everyone who trusts in you.

NET.1: How lovely is the place where you live, O LORD WHO RULES OVER ALL!
NET.2: I desperately want to be in the courts of the LORD’s temple. My heart and my entire being shout for joy to the living God.
NET.3: Even the birds find a home there, and the swallow builds a nest, where she can protect her young near your altars, O LORD WHO RULES OVER ALL, my king and my God.
NET.4: How blessed are those who live in your temple and praise you continually! (Selah)
NET.5: How blessed are those who find their strength in you, and long to travel the roads that lead to your temple!
NET.6: As they pass through the Baca Valley, he provides a spring for them. The rain even covers it with pools of water.
NET.7: They are sustained as they travel along; each one appears before God in Zion.
NET.8: O LORD, sovereign God, hear my prayer! Listen, O God of Jacob! (Selah)
NET.9: O God, take notice of our shield! Show concern for your chosen king!
NET.10: Certainly spending just one day in your temple courts is better than spending a thousand elsewhere. I would rather stand at the entrance to the temple of my God than live in the tents of the wicked.
NET.11: For the LORD God is our sovereign protector. The LORD bestows favor and honor; he withholds no good thing from those who have integrity.
NET.12: O LORD WHO RULES OVER ALL, how blessed are those who trust in you!
